raw_consumer_statement:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am writing to formally file a complaint regarding an unresolved issue with a Best Buy credit card issued by Citibank. I have been charged ongoing monthly interest and payments for over 3 years for a purchase I never kept, and neither Best Buy nor Citibank has provided a clear explanation or resolution despite repeated attempts. 

Summary : In XXXX I purchased a {$2000.00} XXXX from Best Buy using their store credit card ( issued by Citi ). 

I returned the laptop, and received confirmation ( have return receipt, best buy also has it ) at the store that the return was processed. 

Despite this, I have been charged monthly interest and principal amounts since then, totaling hundreds ( or possibly thousands ) of dollars. 

I have not made any other purchases on this card the returned laptop was the only transaction. 

I have contacted Best Buy, who told me they can not help and referred me to Citi. 

I contacted Citi, who claimed they only issue the card and redirected me back to Best Buy. 

Eventually, a Citi representative helped me get on the phone with a third-party service center ( overseas ), who were unable to tell me what the charges were for. 

I requested full transaction history, and received incomplete statements that only show payment and interest, with no details of the original purchase or return. 

I have also personally called the service center multiple times and have not received a clear explanation. 

Why I'm Contacting CFPB : I believe I am being billed for a purchase that was returned, and neither Best Buy nor Citibank is taking responsibility. I am being charged mostly interest, and the total owed never goes down meaningfully. I have no visibility into the original charges that created this ongoing balance, and no one can tell me what the charges are for. 


Sincerely, XXXX
